title: Everlance vs Toggl Track for Solo users
meta_description: Everlance vs Toggl Track for Solo users: Everlance wins by combining mileage and time tracking, while Toggl Track requires separate tools.
constraint_lens: This person wants a single tool that handles everything without needing to maintain multiple apps or workflows.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for Solo users who need to track time and mileage in one place.
one_second_verdict.reason: Toggl Track fails first because it only tracks time and requires a separate tool to log mileage.

verdict.summary: Everlance is the better choice when you need both time and mileage tracking in one system. It automatically tracks trips using GPS and lets you log work time alongside driving activity. Toggl Track only handles time tracking, which forces you to use another app for mileage, creating extra tools to manage.
verdict.decision_rule: If tracking time and mileage requires maintaining separate tools instead of one integrated system, Toggl Track fails first.

sections.heading: Why Everlance fits Solo users better
sections.content: This solo user wants to avoid juggling multiple tools for different parts of their work. Everlance fits because it combines GPS-based mileage tracking with time tracking in one app. That removes the need to switch between apps or maintain separate systems.

bullets.point: Everlance automatically tracks trips using GPS and records mileage in the background.
bullets.why_it_matters: This removes the need to manually log driving distance or use a separate mileage app.

bullets.point: It combines time tracking and mileage tracking within the same system.
bullets.why_it_matters: You can manage both aspects of work in one place, reducing the number of tools you need to maintain.

bullets.point: Trips and work sessions are organized together for reporting and review.
bullets.why_it_matters: This keeps everything aligned without needing to reconcile data from multiple apps.

bullets.point: Toggl Track provides a focused time tracking system with simple timers and reporting.
bullets.why_it_matters: This keeps time tracking clean, but does not cover mileage tracking needs.

bullets.point: It allows detailed tracking of tasks and projects across different work types.
bullets.why_it_matters: This is useful for general work tracking, but requires another tool for driving-related tracking.

bullets.point: Toggl Track works across multiple platforms with consistent time tracking features.
bullets.why_it_matters: This helps with flexibility, but still does not solve mileage tracking within the same system.

items.fails_when: Everlance becomes limiting when you need advanced project-based time tracking with detailed task breakdowns.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Toggl Track if you need deeper project and task-level time tracking.

items.fails_when: Toggl Track breaks down when you need to track mileage but must rely on a separate app alongside time tracking.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Everlance if you want time and mileage tracking in one system.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This could flip if your work does not involve driving and you only need detailed time tracking. In that case, Toggl Track may be a better fit because of its focus on time tracking.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: Pick Everlance if you need to track both time and mileage together.
Pick Toggl Track if you only need time tracking without driving data.
Avoid Toggl Track if you do not want to manage multiple tools.

faqs.q: Why is Everlance better for solo users?
faqs.a: Because it combines time and mileage tracking in one app, reducing the need for multiple tools.

faqs.q: What is missing in Toggl Track for this use case?
faqs.a: It does not include mileage tracking, so you need a separate app for driving work.

faqs.q: Is Toggl Track a bad tool?
faqs.a: No. It is a strong time tracking tool but does not cover mileage tracking needs.

faqs.q: When should I choose Toggl Track instead?
faqs.a: Choose Toggl Track when your work does not involve driving and you only need time tracking.
